title: PCIdep
text: 𝙿𝙲𝙸𝚍𝚎𝚙\texttt{PCIdep} 𝚁\texttt{R} package implementing approaches introduced González-Delgado et al. (2023) perform selective inference hierarchical kk-means clustering observations drawn general matrix normal model: 𝐗∼ℳ𝒩n×p(𝛍,𝐔,𝚺), \mathbf{X}\sim\mathcal{MN}_{n\times p}(\boldsymbol\mu,\mathbf{U},\mathbf{\Sigma}), $`\boldsymbol\mu\\mathcal{M}_{n\times p}(\mathbb{R})`$ $`\mathbf{U}\\mathcal{M}_{n\times n}(\mathbb{R})`$, $`\mathbf{\Sigma}\\mathcal{M}_{p\times p}(\mathbb{R})`$ positive definite matrices encoding dependence structure observations features respectively. 𝙿𝙲𝙸𝚍𝚎𝚙\texttt{PCIdep} natural extension general matrix normal model work clusterpval [2] KMeansInference [3] framework selective inference hierarchical clustering kk-means respectively presented 𝐔=𝐈n\mathbf{U}=\mathbf{}_n 𝚺=σ𝐈p\mathbf{\Sigma}=\sigma\mathbf{}_p, allowing estimation unknown parameter σ\sigma. Inference user-specified clustering algorithm can performed via Monte Carlo approximation.

title: PCIdep
text: selective type error control ensured 𝐔\mathbf{U} Compound Symmetry (CS) structure, , 𝐔=(−b)𝐈n+b𝟏n\mathbf{U} = (-b)\mathbf{}_n + b\mathbf{1}_n, /(n−1)<b<aa/(n-1)<b<. However, method robust 𝐔\mathbf{U} CS deviation large. recommend practitioner use method known 𝐔\mathbf{U} matrices deviate substantially CS structure (e.g. AR(1), Diagonal, Banded Toeplitz, depending parameters).

title: PCIdep
text: 𝙿𝙲𝙸𝚍𝚎𝚙\texttt{PCIdep} can installed using inquires, please file issue contact us.

title: PCIdep
text: function test.clusters.hc adapts framework presented clusterpval general matrix normal model. allows selective inference hierarchical clustering (HC) multiple types linkage. -estimation $`\mathbf{\Sigma}`$ known $`\mathbf{U}`$ possible asymptotically respecting selective type error.

title: PCIdep
code: # Model parameters n <- 50 p <- 20 M <- Matrix::Matrix(0, nrow = n , ncol = p) # Mean matrix Sigma <- stats::toeplitz(seq(1, 0.1, length = p)) # Sigma: dependence between features U <- matrixNormal::I(n) # U: dependence between observations  # Simulate matrix normal samples X <- matrixNormal::rmatnorm(s = 1, M, U, Sigma) Y <- matrixNormal::rmatnorm(s = 1, M, U, Sigma) # i.i.d. copy of X  # HC with average linkage under a global null hypothesis test.clusters.hc(X, U, Sigma, NC = 3, clusters = sample(1:3, 2), plot = TRUE, linkage = "average")  # HC with complete linkage under the global null hypothesis and over-estimation of Sigma test.clusters.hc(X, U, Sigma = NULL, Y = Y, NC = 3, clusters = sample(1:3, 2), plot = TRUE, linkage = "complete")

title: PCIdep
text: function test.clusters.km adapts framework presented KMeansInference general matrix normal model. allows selective inference kk-means clustering. -estimation $`\mathbf{\Sigma}`$ known $`\mathbf{U}`$ possible asymptotically respecting selective type error.

title: PCIdep
code: # Model parameters n <- 50 p <- 20 M <- Matrix::Matrix(0, nrow = n , ncol = p) # Mean matrix Sigma <- stats::toeplitz(seq(1, 0.1, length = p)) # Sigma: dependence between features U <- matrixNormal::I(n) # U: dependence between observations  # Simulate matrix normal samples X <- matrixNormal::rmatnorm(s = 1, M, U, Sigma) Y <- matrixNormal::rmatnorm(s = 1, M, U, Sigma) # i.i.d. copy of X  # k-means under the global null hypothesis test.clusters.km(X, U, Sigma, NC = 3, clusters = sample(1:3, 2))  # k-means under the global null hypothesis and over-estimation of Sigma test.clusters.km(X, U, Sigma = NULL, Y = Y, NC = 3, clusters = sample(1:3, 2))

what: Selective inference after any user-specified clustering algorithm
title: PCIdep
text: function test.clusters.MC adapts framework presented clusterpval general matrix normal model, user-specified clustering algorithm. pp-value computed using Monte Carlo approximation. -estimation $`\mathbf{\Sigma}`$ known $`\mathbf{U}`$ possible asymptotically respecting selective type error.

previous_headings: Selective inference after any user-specified clustering algorithm
title: PCIdep
code: # Model parameters n <- 50 p <- 20 M <- Matrix::Matrix(0, nrow = n , ncol = p) # Mean matrix Sigma <- stats::toeplitz(seq(1, 0.1, length = p)) # Sigma: dependence between features U <- matrixNormal::I(n) # U: dependence between observations  # Simulate matrix normal sample X <- matrixNormal::rmatnorm(s = 1, M, U, Sigma)  # Using HDBSCAN clustering from dbscan package. This algorithm selects automatically the number of clusters NC. # Additional clustering parameters must be set as default values when defining cl_fun. # install.packages('dbscan')  hdbscan.clustering <- function(X, NC = NULL, min.occupancy = 5){   X.clus <- dbscan::hdbscan(X, minPts = min.occupancy)   return(X.clus$cluster + 1) }  # First cluster the data clusters_X <- hdbscan.clustering(X) # Then test for the equality of clusters 3 and 1 test.clusters.MC(X, U = U, Sigma = Sigma, clusters = c(3,1), cl = clusters_X, cl_fun = hdbscan.clustering, NC = NULL, ndraws = 500)

title: PCIdep
text: [1] J. González-Delgado, M. Deronzier, J. Cortés P. Neuvial, Post-clustering Inference Dependence. arXiv:2310.11822, 2023. [2] L. L. Gao, J. Bien, D. Witten. Selective inference hierarchical clustering. Journal American Statistical Association, 119(545):332–342, 2024. [3] Y. T. Chen D. M. Witten. Selective inference k-means clustering. Journal Machine Learning Research, 24(152):1–41, 2023.

title: Test for the difference of two cluster means after any clustering algorithm, for matrix normal model with arbitrary scale matrices. — test.clusters.MC
text: X \(n \times p\) matrix drawn \(n \times p\) matrix normal distribution \(\mathcal{MN}(\)M, U, Sigma\()\). X must \(n\) rows \(p\) columns. U \(n \times n\) positive-definite matrix describing dependence structure rows X. NULL, observations considered independent U set \(n \times n\) identity matrix. Sigma \(p \times p\) positive-definite matrix describing dependence structure columns X. NULL, Sigma -estimated (sense Loewner partial order). Y Sigma NULL, ..d. copy X allowing estimation. Y must number columns X. UY Sigma NULL, positive-definite matrix describing dependence structure rows Y. NULL inverse provided, set identity matrix default. precUY inverse matrix UY, can provided increase computational efficiency. UY NULL precUY NULL, precUY obtained inverting UY. clusters vector two integers 1 NC indicating pair clusters whose means compared. cl_fun function returning assignments clusters. function must take input data matrix X number clusters NC. NC number clusters choose, passed argument cl_fun. Must set NULL required cl_fun. cl result clustering X using cl_fun. can useful precompute quantity choosing clusters. ndraws number Monte Carlo iterations.

title: Test for the difference of two cluster means after any clustering algorithm, for matrix normal model with arbitrary scale matrices. — test.clusters.MC
text: pvalue - p-value difference cluster means. stat - test statistic. stdrr - Monte Carlo standard error. clusters - partition n observations retrieved clustering algorithm.

title: Test for the difference of two cluster means after any clustering algorithm, for matrix normal model with arbitrary scale matrices. — test.clusters.MC
text: [1] L. L. Gao, J. Bien, D. Witten. Selective inference hierarchical clustering. Journal American Statistical Association, 0(0):1–11, 2022.

title: Test for the difference of two cluster means after any clustering algorithm, for matrix normal model with arbitrary scale matrices. — test.clusters.MC
code: n <- 50 p <- 20 M <- Matrix::Matrix(0, nrow = n , ncol = p) # Mean matrix Sigma <- stats::toeplitz(seq(1, 0.1, length = p)) # Sigma: dependence between features U <- matrixNormal::I(n) # U: dependence between observations X <- matrixNormal::rmatnorm(s = 1, M, U, Sigma) Y <- matrixNormal::rmatnorm(s = 1, M, U, Sigma) # i.i.d. copy of X  # Using HDBSCAN clustering from dbscan package. This algorithm selects  # automatically the number of clusters NC. # Additional clustering parameters must be set as default values # when defining cl_fun.  # install.packages('dbscan')  hdbscan.clustering <- function(X, NC = NULL, min.occupancy = 5){    X.clus <- dbscan::hdbscan(X, minPts = min.occupancy)  return(X.clus$cluster + 1)   }  # We start by clustering the data clusters_X <- hdbscan.clustering(X) #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘dbscan’ # We test for the equality of clusters 3 and 1 test.clusters.MC(X, U = U, Sigma = Sigma, clusters = c(3,1),  cl = clusters_X, cl_fun = hdbscan.clustering, NC = NULL, ndraws = 500) #> Error: object 'clusters_X' not found
